cluster_fsm.spec_reference: Matter 1.5 Application Cluster Specification, Section 9.3, Pages 716-742

features.name: ChargingPreferences
features.description: EVSE supports storing user charging preferences (mandatory)

features.name: SoCReporting
features.description: EVSE supports reporting of vehicle State of Charge

features.name: PlugAndCharge
features.description: EVSE supports PLC to support Plug and Charge; requires compatible EV

features.name: RFID
features.description: EVSE is fitted with an RFID reader for user/vehicle identification

features.name: V2X
features.description: EVSE supports bi-directional charging and discharging

dependencies.description: Server side SHALL depend on setting time from another device or using a real-time clock; Time Synchronization cluster recommended for Epoch timestamps in events and attributes
EVSE devices supporting PFR/FA features of Device Energy Management interact for charging profile control; EMS may use PA feature to adjust charge/discharge current
Optimizer may use Commodity Price cluster for cheapest/lowest-GHG charging schedule

states.name: NotPluggedIn
states.description: No EV is connected to the EVSE. Idle state awaiting vehicle connection.

states.name: PluggedInNoDemand
states.description: EV is physically connected but not demanding current from the EVSE.
states.safety_implications: EV connected but not charging; pilot signal negotiation may be in progress

states.name: PluggedInDemand
states.description: EV is connected and demanding current, but EVSE is not currently allowing current flow.
states.safety_implications: EV is ready and waiting for EVSE authorization; no energy transfer occurring

states.name: PluggedInCharging
states.description: EV is connected and actively charging; current is flowing from EVSE to EV.
states.safety_implications: Active high-current energy transfer; overcurrent, overvoltage, ground fault conditions are safety-critical

states.name: PluggedInDischarging
states.description: EV is connected and actively discharging (V2G/V2H); current is flowing from EV to EVSE/grid.
states.safety_implications: Bi-directional power flow; backfeed to grid or home; requires compatible EV and contactor safety interlocks

states.name: SessionEnding
states.description: Transitional state: EVSE is ending any plugged-in session, preparing to return to NotPluggedIn. May take a few seconds for cleanup.
states.safety_implications: Contactors opening; current must cease before physical disconnect to prevent arcing

states.name: Fault
states.description: EVSE has detected a hardware or electrical fault. Further details in FaultState attribute.
states.safety_implications: Critical safety state. Charging and discharging MUST be prevented. Some faults (GFCI, CCID) require physical operator intervention (e.g., button on equipment or breaker panel) to clear.

states.name: Diagnostics
states.description: EVSE is running self-diagnostics initiated by StartDiagnostics command. Charging/discharging not permitted.
states.safety_implications: Charging suspended during diagnostics; may involve internal electrical self-tests

transitions.guard_condition: EV physically connected AND pilot signal detected
EV pilot signal indicates current demand (J1772/IEC61851 state change)
EV pilot signal no longer indicates demand
SupplyState == Disabled AND FaultState == NoError AND SupplyState != DisabledDiagnostics AND ChargingEnabledUntil is null OR ChargingEnabledUntil > current_time
actions: generate_event(EnergyTransferStarted, {SessionID: SessionID, State: PluggedInCharging, MaximumCurrent: MaximumChargeCurrent})
transitions.timing_constraint: ChargingEnabledUntil sets auto-stop time; timer_ChargingEnabledUntil started if not null

error_responses.description: Error present on EVSE; charging cannot be enabled

transitions.guard_condition: SupplyState == DischargingEnabled AND Feature_V2X == true AND FaultState == NoError AND SupplyState != DisabledDiagnostics
actions: generate_event(EnergyTransferStarted, {SessionID: SessionID, State: PluggedInCharging, MaximumCurrent: MaximumChargeCurrent})

error_responses.description: Error present on EVSE; charging cannot be enabled

transitions.spec_reference: Section 9.3.9.2.4 'Effect on Receipt' EnableCharging (previously DischargingEnabled -> Enabled)

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_V2X == true AND SupplyState == Disabled AND FaultState == NoError AND SupplyState != DisabledDiagnostics AND (DischargingEnabledUntil is null OR DischargingEnabledUntil > current_time)
actions: generate_event(EnergyTransferStarted, {SessionID: SessionID, State: PluggedInDischarging, MaximumCurrent: 0, MaximumDischargeCurrent: MaximumDischargeCurrent})
transitions.timing_constraint: DischargingEnabledUntil sets auto-stop time; timer_DischargingEnabledUntil started if not null

error_responses.description: Error present on EVSE; discharging cannot be enabled
V2X feature not supported; EnableDischarging unavailable

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_V2X == true AND SupplyState == ChargingEnabled AND FaultState == NoError AND SupplyState != DisabledDiagnostics
actions: generate_event(EnergyTransferStarted, {SessionID: SessionID, State: PluggedInDischarging, MaximumCurrent: MaximumChargeCurrent, MaximumDischargeCurrent: MaximumDischargeCurrent})

error_responses.description: Error present on EVSE; discharging cannot be enabled

transitions.spec_reference: Section 9.3.9.3.3 'Effect on Receipt' EnableDischarging (previously ChargingEnabled -> Enabled)

transitions.guard_condition: SupplyState != Disabled
actions: generate_event(EnergyTransferStopped, {SessionID: SessionID, State: PluggedInDemand, Reason: EVSEStopped, EnergyTransferred: SessionEnergyCharged})

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_V2X == true AND SupplyState != Disabled
actions: generate_event(EnergyTransferStopped, {SessionID: SessionID, State: PluggedInDemand, Reason: EVSEStopped, EnergyTransferred: SessionEnergyCharged, EnergyDischarged: SessionEnergyDischarged})

transitions.guard_condition: SupplyState == Disabled
ChargingEnabledUntil != null AND current_time >= ChargingEnabledUntil AND DischargingEnabledUntil <= current_time OR DischargingEnabledUntil == null_past
actions: generate_event(EnergyTransferStopped, {SessionID: SessionID, State: PluggedInDemand, Reason: EVSEStopped, EnergyTransferred: SessionEnergyCharged})
transitions.timing_constraint: Auto-triggers when ChargingEnabledUntil timestamp is reached

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_V2X == true AND ChargingEnabledUntil != null AND current_time >= ChargingEnabledUntil AND DischargingEnabledUntil > current_time OR DischargingEnabledUntil == null
transitions.timing_constraint: Auto-triggers when ChargingEnabledUntil expires but DischargingEnabledUntil is still active
transitions.spec_reference: Section 9.3.9.2.4 'Effect on Receipt' EnableCharging (DischargingEnabled if DischargingEnabledUntil in future or null)

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_V2X == true AND DischargingEnabledUntil != null AND current_time >= DischargingEnabledUntil AND ChargingEnabledUntil <= current_time OR ChargingEnabledUntil is past
actions: generate_event(EnergyTransferStopped, {SessionID: SessionID, State: PluggedInDemand, Reason: EVSEStopped, EnergyTransferred: SessionEnergyCharged, EnergyDischarged: SessionEnergyDischarged})
transitions.timing_constraint: Auto-triggers when DischargingEnabledUntil timestamp is reached

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_V2X == true AND DischargingEnabledUntil != null AND current_time >= DischargingEnabledUntil AND ChargingEnabledUntil > current_time OR ChargingEnabledUntil == null
transitions.timing_constraint: Auto-triggers when DischargingEnabledUntil expires but ChargingEnabledUntil is still active
transitions.spec_reference: Section 9.3.9.3.3 'Effect on Receipt' EnableDischarging (ChargingEnabled if ChargingEnabledUntil in future or null)

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_PREF == true AND all ChargingTargets are within valid range AND each day of week is in at most one ChargingTargetSchedule AND total targets <= device_maximum

error_responses.condition: any charging target out of valid range OR a day of week appears in more than one ChargingTargetSchedule
error_responses.description: Invalid charging targets or duplicate day assignment

error_responses.condition: total number of charging targets exceeds device maximum
error_responses.description: Device cannot store the requested number of charging targets

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_PREF == true
Feature_PREF == true
SupplyState == Disabled

error_responses.description: EVSE can only enter diagnostics from Disabled SupplyState

transitions.guard_condition: manufacturer_diagnostics_complete == true
transitions.timing_constraint: Diagnostics duration is at manufacturer's discretion

transitions.guard_condition: FaultState != NoError AND fault detected by EVSE hardware or pilot signal monitoring
actions: generate_event(Fault, {SessionID: SessionID, State: Fault, FaultStatePreviousState: previous_FaultState, CurrentFaultState: FaultState})

transitions.guard_condition: fault condition resolved (possibly by physical operator action for GFCI/CCID faults)
transitions.timing_constraint: Some faults require physical operator intervention before clearing
transitions.spec_reference: Section 9.3 'Safety mechanism that may lockout remote operation until initial latching conditions met'

transitions.guard_condition: EV physically disconnected AND contactors safely opened
actions: generate_event(EVNotDetected, {SessionID: SessionID, State: SessionEnding, SessionDuration: SessionDuration, SessionEnergyCharged: SessionEnergyCharged, SessionEnergyDischarged: SessionEnergyDischarged})

transitions.guard_condition: EV cable disconnection detected by pilot signal monitoring
actions: generate_event(EnergyTransferStopped, {SessionID: SessionID, State: SessionEnding, Reason: Other, EnergyTransferred: SessionEnergyCharged})
transitions.timing_constraint: SessionEnding is a transitional state that may last a few seconds
transitions.spec_reference: Section 9.3.8.1 'State Attribute' NOTE: SessionEnding is a transition period

transitions.guard_condition: EV pilot signal shows charging complete or EV-side stop
actions: generate_event(EnergyTransferStopped, {SessionID: SessionID, State: PluggedInNoDemand, Reason: EVStopped, EnergyTransferred: SessionEnergyCharged})

transitions.guard_condition: MinimumChargeCurrent <= new_value <= MaximumChargeCurrent
transitions.timing_constraint: Value SHALL be persisted across reboots

transitions.guard_condition: 0 <= new_value <= 86400
transitions.timing_constraint: Default 600s; mandated in some markets (e.g. UK)

error_responses.description: Value exceeds maximum constraint of 86400 seconds

events.name: EVConnected

fields.name: SessionID
fields.description: The new session ID created after the vehicle is plugged in

events.description: Generated when the EV is plugged in and a new session begins

events.name: EVNotDetected

fields.name: SessionID
fields.description: Current value of the SessionID attribute

fields.name: State
fields.description: Value of the State attribute prior to EV not being detected

fields.name: SessionDuration
fields.description: Total duration of the session from plug-in to unplug

fields.name: SessionEnergyCharged
fields.description: Total energy transferred from EVSE to EV during session; NOT a net value

fields.name: SessionEnergyDischarged
fields.description: Total energy transferred from EV to EVSE during session; V2X conformance; NOT a net value

events.description: Generated when EV is unplugged or not detected after previously being plugged in; ends the session

events.name: EnergyTransferStarted

fields.name: SessionID
fields.description: Value of SessionID attribute at event generation time

fields.name: State
fields.description: Value of State attribute at event generation time (PluggedInCharging or PluggedInDischarging)

fields.name: MaximumCurrent
fields.description: Maximum charging current; 0 if EV not enabled for charging

fields.name: MaximumDischargeCurrent
fields.description: Maximum discharging current; 0 if EV not enabled for discharging; V2X conformance

events.description: Generated whenever EV starts charging or discharging, EXCEPT when switching between charge/discharge modes under PowerAdjustment feature control

events.name: EnergyTransferStopped

fields.name: SessionID
fields.description: Value of SessionID attribute prior to energy transfer stopping

fields.name: State
fields.description: Value of State attribute prior to energy transfer stopping

fields.name: Reason
fields.description: Reason for energy transfer stopping: EVStopped(0), EVSEStopped(1), Other(2)

fields.name: EnergyTransferred
fields.description: Total energy charged during this transfer period

fields.name: EnergyDischarged
fields.description: Total energy discharged during this transfer period; V2X conformance

events.description: Generated whenever EV stops charging or discharging, EXCEPT when switching between charge/discharge modes under PowerAdjustment feature control

events.name: Fault

fields.name: SessionID
fields.description: Current SessionID when fault occurred (null if no session)

fields.name: State
fields.description: Current State value (Fault)

fields.name: FaultStatePreviousState
fields.description: Previous FaultState before this fault event

fields.name: CurrentFaultState
fields.description: Current FaultState value indicating type of fault detected

events.description: Critical priority event generated when a fault is detected by the EVSE internally or via pilot signal monitoring

events.name: RFID

fields.name: UID
fields.description: RFID card UID read by the EVSE reader

events.description: Generated when user taps RFID card on reader; must be subscribed to by EVSE Management cluster client for authorization; RFID feature conformance only

timers.name: timer_ChargingEnabledUntil
timers.trigger: Set when EnableCharging command is received with non-null ChargingEnabledUntil field
timers.expiry_transition: T011 (to Disabled) or T012 (to DischargingEnabled if V2X active)

timers.name: timer_DischargingEnabledUntil
timers.trigger: Set when EnableDischarging command is received with non-null DischargingEnabledUntil field; V2X feature only
timers.expiry_transition: T013 (to Disabled) or T014 (to ChargingEnabled if charging active)

timers.name: timer_RandomizationDelay
timers.trigger: Computed random delay (0 to RandomizationDelayWindow-1 seconds) applied before scheduled charge start
timers.expiry_transition: Automatic charging schedule start after delay expires

functions.name: update_NextChargeStartTime
functions.description: Recomputes NextChargeStartTime attribute based on current ChargingTargetSchedules, MaximumChargeCurrent, local grid voltage, RandomizationDelayWindow, and current time. Sets to null if no scheduled charging or vehicle not plugged in with charging enabled.

functions.name: update_NextChargeTargetTime
functions.description: Recomputes NextChargeTargetTime attribute from active ChargingTargetSchedules. Returns null if no scheduled charging or EVSE Mode is Manual.

functions.name: update_NextChargeRequiredEnergy
functions.description: Recomputes NextChargeRequiredEnergy from active ChargingTargetSchedule's AddedEnergy target. Returns null if no scheduled charging or if TargetSoC is the active target.

functions.name: update_NextChargeTargetSoC
functions.description: Recomputes NextChargeTargetSoC from active ChargingTargetSchedule's TargetSoC. Returns null if no scheduled charging or if AddedEnergy is the active target. If SOC feature not supported, only null and 100% are valid.

functions.name: stop_automatic_charging_if_active
functions.description: If EVSE is currently charging based on automatic schedule mode (not manual), stops charging after ClearTargets removes all weekly targets.

functions.name: compute_MaximumChargeCurrent

parameters.name: CableAssemblyCurrentLimit
parameters.description: Detected from cable assembly at plug-in

functions.description: Returns the minimum of CircuitCapacity, CableAssemblyCurrentLimit, command MaximumChargeCurrent, and UserMaximumChargeCurrent. This is the actual maximum offered to the EV.

functions.name: compute_MaximumDischargeCurrent

parameters.name: CableAssemblyCurrentLimit
parameters.description: Detected from cable assembly at plug-in

functions.description: Returns the minimum of CircuitCapacity, CableAssemblyCurrentLimit, and command MaximumDischargeCurrent. V2X feature only.

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Electrical faults including GroundFault, LiveNeutralSwap, OverCurrent, ContactWetFailure, and EmergencyStop represent direct risk of electrocution, fire, or equipment damage
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: SupplyState forced to DisabledError; some faults (GFCI/CCID) require physical operator intervention to clear; remote operation locked out until conditions met

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: EnableCharging can initiate high-current flow to vehicle; misconfigured MaximumChargeCurrent could exceed circuit or cable limits
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: MaximumChargeCurrent computed as minimum of CircuitCapacity, CableAssemblyCurrentLimit, command value, and UserMaximumChargeCurrent

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: EnableDischarging allows backfeed from EV to home/grid; uncontrolled backfeed is a safety hazard for utility workers and home wiring
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: V2X feature must be explicitly supported; MaximumDischargeCurrent bounded by CircuitCapacity and CableAssemblyCurrentLimit; requires compatible EV

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Fault detection must be reliable; delayed or missed fault detection (e.g., GFCI, GroundFault) could allow dangerous current to flow
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Fault event is CRITICAL priority; SupplyState immediately set to DisabledError on fault detection

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Premature fault clearance (e.g., software reset without physical inspection) could restore charging after a GFCI trip without addressing root cause
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Spec requires physical operator intervention for some faults (GFCI/CCID); however no mandatory remote lockout mechanism is defined in the cluster

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: If CircuitCapacity is set too high (e.g., by a remote Manage-level command after installation), it could allow overcurrent beyond the physical circuit breaker rating
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Intended to be set by installer; however no mandatory installer-only access restriction is defined in the cluster spec

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Contactors must be safely opened before physical cable disconnection to prevent arcing; the transition period must ensure safe hardware sequencing
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: SessionEnding is explicitly noted as a transitional state that may take a few seconds; hardware safety sequencing is manufacturer-dependent

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: If RandomizationDelayWindow is set to 0, all EVSEs in a grid region may start simultaneously at cheap rate start time, causing grid demand surge
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Default 600s recommended; mandated by some markets (UK); RW VM access requires Manage privilege

spec_gaps.description: StartDiagnostics: Duration of diagnostics is 'at the discretion of the manufacturer'. No timeout or maximum duration is specified. An EVSE could remain in DisabledDiagnostics indefinitely.
spec_gaps.security_impact: A malicious Operate-level actor could keep the EVSE in diagnostics mode indefinitely, preventing legitimate charging sessions (DoS)

spec_gaps.description: CircuitCapacity attribute has no defined maximum constraint and no specification of who can set it post-installation. The spec says 'assumed that the device will allow the setting of such values by an installer' but no access control attribute or installer role is defined in the cluster.
spec_gaps.security_impact: A Manage-level remote actor could set CircuitCapacity to an arbitrarily high value, potentially enabling overcurrent beyond physical safety limits

spec_gaps.description: The 'safety mechanism that may lockout remote operation until initial latching conditions have been met' is described in the cluster introduction but is not formally defined as an attribute, state, or command. No mechanism exists to query or verify lockout status remotely.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Implementers may omit the physical-interlock safety mechanism with no cluster-level way to detect or enforce it

spec_gaps.description: The Fault event field structure is described in the events table but the exact field names are not specified in the spec excerpt (unlike other events). FaultStatePreviousState field name is inferred.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Inconsistent fault event field naming across implementations could prevent proper log analysis for security incident investigation

spec_gaps.description: SetTargets command stores charging targets but the spec uses SHOULD for automatic charging behavior: 'should be able to compute the latest NextChargeStartTime' and 'should automatically begin charging'. This is not a SHALL requirement.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Inconsistent automatic charging behavior across implementations; schedule-based attacks may not be uniformly mitigable

spec_gaps.description: RFID event authorization: 'The lookup and authorization of RFID UID is outside the scope of this cluster.' No mechanism within the cluster prevents unauthorized charging sessions if the RFID authorization system is compromised or unavailable.
spec_gaps.security_impact: If RFID authorization backend is offline or bypassed, EVSE authorization decision is undefined at the cluster level

spec_gaps.description: SessionID is allowed to roll over. No mechanism prevents wrap-around SessionID from being mistaken for a previous session. No uniqueness guarantee beyond 'unlikely in EVSE lifetime unless electrical fault'.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Rapid session cycling (e.g., due to electrical fault) could cause SessionID rollover, leading to log confusion or session confusion for billing/energy tracking

spec_gaps.description: RandomizationDelayWindow can be written by Manage-level access. No restriction prevents setting it to 0 remotely, eliminating grid-mandated demand management randomization.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Remote attacker with Manage privilege could disable randomization, enabling coordinated grid demand surge attack across a fleet of EVSEs

spec_gaps.description: EnableCharging and EnableDischarging effect on receipt: when SupplyState is already in a partially enabled state (e.g., calling EnableCharging when SupplyState==ChargingEnabled), the spec does not explicitly state whether the command updates existing parameters or is rejected.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Implicit update behavior could allow an Operate-level actor to reduce minimum current limits or change expiry times on an active session without triggering a new EnergyTransferStarted event
